title: Week 1 Population Pyramid
text:
2. Load WPP 2024 data

data(popAge5dt)

country_name <- "Republic of Korea"
target_year <- 2020

title: Week 1 Population Pyramid
text:
3. Create population pyramid data

pyramid_data <- popAge5dt %>%
  filter(name == country_name, year == target_year) %>%
  select(year, age, popM, popF) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(popM, popF),
    names_to = "sex",
    values_to = "population"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex = case_when(
      sex == "popM" ~ "남",
      sex == "popF" ~ "여"
    ),
    age = factor(age, levels = unique(age)),
    plot_pop = ifelse(sex == "남", -population, population)
  )

total_pop <- sum(pyramid_data$population) * 1000

title: Week 1 Population Pyramid
text:
4. Population pyramid

ggplot(
  pyramid_data,
  aes(x = age, y = plot_pop, fill = sex)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) format(abs(x), big.mark = ",")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "성별",
    values = c(
      "남" = "#00AFC4",
      "여" = "#006D6F"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "대한민국",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "총인구: ",
      format(round(total_pop), big.mark = ","),
      "명 (",
      target_year,
      "년)"
    ),
    x = NULL,
    y = "인구수(천명)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

title: Global Health and Demography
text:
Course objectives
By the end of this course, students will be able to:
1) Understand how demographic change shapes global health challenges and opportunities
2) Analyze population and health data using R and GeoDa
3) Explain and demonstrate the health consequences of demographic transitions
Course Schedule
1) Week 1 (May 26): Introduction to Global Health and Demography
2) Week 2 (June 9): Contemporary Fertility and Mortality Transition
3) Week 3 (June 16): Mobility, Migration, and Health
4) Week 4 (June 23): Spatial Demography and Health

title: Global Health and Demography
text:
Course objectives
By the end of this course, students will be able to:
1) Understand how demographic change shapes global health challenges and opportunities
2) Analyze population and health data using R and GeoDa
3) Explain and demonstrate the health consequences of demographic transitions
Course Schedule
1) Week 1 (May 26): Introduction to Global Health and Demography
2) Week 2 (June 9): Contemporary Fertility and Mortality Transition
3) Week 3 (June 16): Mobility, Migration, and Health
4) Week 4 (June 23): Spatial Demography and Health

title: Global Health and Demography
text:
II. Lecture materials

Lecture slides will be shared via email before each class. Please check your email.
Course materials and practical tutorials are available at:
https://myunggu82.github.io/global-health-demography/

title: Global Health and Demography
text:
III. Evaluation

평가: P/F (출석 30%, 평가 70%)
출석: 수업 일수 1/2 (2번) 이상 결석한 경우, 사유와 상관없이 F학점 부여 (서울의대 선택교과 출결 원칙 2020. 실행)
최종성찰보고서

학생 작성 시기: 과목 종료 후 (마지막 수업 후)
양식: A4 한장 이내
제출기한: 마지막 수업 당일 까지 (최종 제출 기한은 ETL 통해 일괄 공지 예정)

title: Global Health and Demography
text:
IV. Reading materials (Option)

팩트풀니스 (한스로슬링, 2018)
죽음의 역사 (앤드류 도이그, 2023)
출산, 그 놀라운 역사 (티나 캐시디, 2007)
How Population Change Will Transform Our World (Sarah Harper, 2016)
Why Demography Matters (Danny Dorling, Stuart Gietel-Basten ,2018)

title: Global Health and Demography
text:
V. Contacts

Prof. Young Su Park (youngsupark@snu.ac.kr)
Dr. Myunggu Jung (myunggu82@snu.ac.kr)
